video_title: Top #DDD Videos in San Antonio with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network

restaurants.name: Wild Barley
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm here in the Alamo Heights area of San Antonio, Texas. And what is that they say about Texas? It's always bigger in Texas. Well, I hear about these two guys, and they got a joint where they're making everything big. Let's see, they're making their beer, they're making their bread, they're making their pizza dough. This is Wild Barley.

dishes.description: Sourdough dark rye and light rye bread, house-made pastrami (brined 14 days, smoked), house-made sauerkraut (fermented 14-21 days), Sputnik sauce, and Swiss cheese.
dishes.guy_response: The pastrami cut that way is genius. Great texture. Great chew. The sauerkraut is not overpowering. Out of this world, Russian dressing. All day long, bro. Delicious. Pastrami is thick. It's sweet. Smoky flavor. It's a game changer.

restaurants.name: Tip Top Cafe
restaurants.guy_intro: You know, comfort food may sound simple, but there's a real art to getting it right and to keeping it that way, which is what three generations have been doing for 70 years here in San Antonio, Texas, at the Tip Top Cafe.

dishes.description: Pork shoulder (Boston butt) marinated overnight in a blend of brown sugar, celery salt, mustard, garlic powder, ginger, cider vinegar, liquid smoke, Worcestershire sauce, soy sauce, oil, black pepper, and rosemary. Roasted at 350 degrees for 3.5-4 hours. Served on toast with brown gravy and mashed potatoes.
dishes.guy_response: That puts the shama-lama in ding-dong. I mean, look at it. It just falls off the bone. Awesome. It was wonderful.

restaurants.guy_intro: Wait, wait, wait, wait, wait, wait, wait, wait. That's a big empanada.

dishes.description: Savory empanada dough (flour, water, salt, milk, butter, egg) filled with sofrito (oil, onion, green/red bell pepper, salt, black pepper, crushed pepper, garlic, oregano), corn, parmesan cheese, and mozzarella. Baked for 4 minutes.
Tenderized pork shoulder, double-dredged in seasoned flour (black pepper, salt, onion powder, paprika, garlic, creole seasoning), then fried. Served with red beans and rice.
dishes.guy_response: Good chop. This is a lot of flavor and just a little bit of batter. All the red beans and rice are good. Oh, you do that right. That's a meal by itself.

restaurants.name: The Jerky Shack
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm here in San Antonio, Texas with the one and only, the legend, Bruce McGill. Here's the story. of San Antonio, and, well, she started as a little shack, hence the name. This is the Jerky Shack.

dishes.description: Chicken brined in salt, sugar, oranges, lemons, habaneros, garlic, bay leaves, fresh thyme, peppercorn. Marinated in roasted vegetables, fresh thyme, brown sugar, black peppercorns, red wine vinegar, molasses, orange/lemon peels, habaneros, ginger, salt, and pepper. Grilled for 20-25 minutes and served with house-made jerk barbecue sauce.
dishes.guy_response: Your technique's great. Oh, man. Barbecue sauce is spot on. You put enough char on this, you get the smokiness. There's great depth of flavor all the way through this. Great spice, not too much. Oh, it's good.

dishes.description: Made with margarine, onions, heavy cream, half and half, whole milk, cheddar, mozzarella, and Colby Jack cheese, mixed with noodles. Seasoned with all-purpose seasoning, salt, and pepper, then baked.
dishes.guy_response: The flavors are so bold in the mac and cheese. Oh, so good.

restaurants.guy_intro: Here in San Antonio, Texas, with some Triple D royalty. That's right. This is the son of Ho Dad's Shane Harden. Now, Shane, you're from San Diego. You love Mexican food. Oh, yeah. Fantastic, I'm taking you to Mexican food. But this isn't regular Mexican food. This is Mexican food that's been chefed a bit.

dishes.description: A sandwich made with house-made birote bread, filled with carnitas (pork butts cooked in manteca), and refried beans. Drowned in a sweet tomato salsa (salsa dulce) and served with a house-made hot sauce, cabbage, radish, pickled red onions, and lime.
dishes.guy_response: This is outrageous. Mm. Mm. That roll is outstanding. That is such a quintessential part to this. The salty, the sweet, the crunch, perfect. I can chug the hot sauce. It's incredible. The beans are ridiculous. Perfect amount of creaminess. But that right there. It's the best. I mean, it is the best. The carnitas are amazing.

restaurants.name: The Co.
restaurants.guy_intro: So here I am at San Antonio, Texas. And you know, the only food I've ever got at a car wash has come out of a vending machine. So you got to know I was totally blown away. To find out connected to this car wash is a totally legit restaurant called The Co.

dishes.description: Lamb patty seasoned with cumin, coriander, whole black pepper, fresh garlic, cayenne, and kosher salt. Grilled and served on a ciabatta roll with a sauce made from ancho, pasilla, and puya peppers, lime juice, olive oil, garlic, kosher salt, sour cream, and heavy cream. Topped with cotija cheese, sliced red onion, tomato, and baby greens.
dishes.guy_response: That's like a milkshake in a bun, man. Mm. Mm. Mm. I need like a blanket. Some of the best lamb burger I've ever had.

restaurants.guy_intro: chuletas can-can. So these are brined overnight.

dishes.description: Pork chops brined overnight, seasoned with salt, oregano, garlic salt, sofrito, oil, and annatto. Baked for 15 minutes at 350 degrees, then fried to order. Served with white rice, sombrecao (beans with pumpkin), sweet plantains, and chimichurri.
dishes.guy_response: All together at prime time. Fantastic.

restaurants.name: Taco Taco
restaurants.guy_intro: You know, I love the names of the restaurants we visit on Triple D. I mean, sometimes it talks about the kind of food they make or who they are or where they're from, and sometimes it just is what it is. The name sounds cool, like Pac Pac or Nico Nicos or Tap Tap. Or here in San Antonio, Texas, where they're serving up some righteous Mexican food, this is Taco Taco.

dishes.description: Puffy taco shell made from corn flour, sugar, salt, butter, and water, flattened and fried. Filled with shredded chicken (chicken thighs boiled with chicken base, cumin, fresh garlic, salt, onions, celery, bell peppers, tomatoes, and tomato sauce), lettuce, tomatoes, shredded cheese, and guacamole.
dishes.guy_response: Puffy ridiculous. The taco's got good crunch, but it's nice and soft. Oh, the chicken's got lots of flavor. You give a huge portion. You know what's the name of this place? Taco Taco Cafe. That's a great name. It's so nice they named it twice. She wins. Good job. Puffy taco. The shell is excellent. Puffed up corn, the tortilla is fabulous. This is a made for me.

restaurants.name: Magnolia Pancake House
restaurants.guy_intro: Another culinary TV movie. Hotel restaurant vet, been cooking since he was 13 years old, returns to San Antonio, Texas to open the mega breakfast and lunch joint, serving over 20,000 scratch meals a month. This is Magnolia Pancake House.

dishes.description: Certified black Angus brisket brined for 14 days in a savory mixture. Cooked with onions, pickling spice, and garlic, then cooled, chopped, and mixed with a garlic/onion puree and potatoes. Cooked on a saute pan until crispy.
dishes.guy_response: Mm. It's an orchestra of flavor. It is the way it's supposed to be done. This is like hearing Metallica rock with the San Francisco Orchestra. Mm. It's hard and heavy hitting, big flavor, and just smooth, comfortable, underlying. I mean, it's just, I don't know what to say. It's awkward. The, the, the, sorry, man. It's OK, guy. Heavenly. Heavenly hash. Super junk, the corned beef. It's cooked perfect. It kind of melts in your mouth.
